title: hash_32 key not used in lichen-link/src/identity.rs or sample lora_ping packet_hash
description: Spec claims Rust lichen-link/src/identity.rs:22 and samples/lora_ping:38 use hash_32 with LICHEN key=0x4c494348454e as initializer. But identity.rs uses SHA256 for IID (no crc), sample uses plain crc32_ieee(data, len) with no key/seed. link_ctx.c:96 tuple_crc also plain. Channel selection pseudocode in 02a-coordinated-capacity.md assumes it. Mismatch in short-addr derivation and channel hash. Tagged codereview.
close_reason: Implemented hash_32(LICHEN_KEY=0x4c494348454e) in lichen-link/src/identity.rs:48. Updated sample lora_ping packet_hash:40 and link_ctx.c tuple_crc to match spec for short-addr/channel (replaced crc32_ieee/SHA256 mismatches). 3 codereview passes per chunk with all findings filed/fixed as codereview beads. All clean.
